exemplar.title: "How can mathematical modeling optimize muscle building by analyzing exercise routines, nutrition, and muscle hypertrophy?"

detailedExemplarData.title: "How can mathematical modeling optimize muscle building by analyzing exercise routines, nutrition, and muscle hypertrophy?"

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Title and introduction clearly communicate the purpose (âOptimizing Muscle Building Using Mathematicsâ).
Research question is explicitly stated and consistently referenced throughout.
Headings and figures are well-organized, making the investigation easy to follow.
weaknesses: Scope remains broad across exercise, nutrition and recovery, diluting focus.
Rationale for selecting specific mathematical models (linear vs exponential vs system) is not justified.
Data sources for recovery modeling are vague, limiting methodological rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work in the 3â4 band. Communication of topic and purpose is effective, the question is clear though somewhat broad, and the methodology is mostly complete but lacks evidence of informed selection. A holistic score of 4/6 reflects competent performance with room to tighten focus and justify model choices.

strengths: Correct application of linear, exponential, logarithmic and system-of-equations models.
Generally accurate use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., âmuscle hypertrophy,â âmTOR,â âlinear regressionâ).
Notation and mathematical vocabulary are mostly consistent and understandable.
weaknesses: Derivations are superficial and coefficients lack empirical justification or statistical validation.
Some variables are undefined and units are inconsistently applied.
Physiological explanations (e.g., mTOR pathway) lack sufficient citation support.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates solid mid-band performance (3â4) in applying knowledge and terminology. While models are used appropriately, derivations and source rigor are limited. A score of 3/6 acknowledges generally good application but points to the need for deeper justification and consistency.

strengths: Research draws on relevant peer-reviewed studies and integrates classical differential equation models.
Analysis connects model outputs to practical training advice and acknowledges diminishing returns.
Discussion recognizes limitations (fabricated data, need for machine learning) and offers a coherent narrative.
weaknesses: Case studies are invented and lack sample details or statistical significance.
Key assumptions (linearity of recovery, fixed growth coefficients) are not explicitly stated or critiqued.
Evaluation remains partly descriptive, with limited depth in critical appraisal of methods.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Critical Thinking fall in the 4â6 band at its lower end. Research is mostly relevant, analysis is logical but relies on assumed parameters, and evaluation is reasoned yet not fully critical. A holistic score of 4/12 accurately reflects moderate critical engagement with clear pathways for deeper justification and critique.

strengths: Report follows a clear Extended Essay structure with title page, contents, body and bibliography.
Equations and figures are properly labeled; word count and page numbering are supplied.
Layout is neat, with consistent font and adequate spacing supporting readability.
weaknesses: No abstract is provided before the table of contents, which is conventional.
Page numbering style (overlined numerals) is non-standard.
Minor formatting inconsistencies (variable bolding, figure resolution issues).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the top band (3â4) in structure and formatting but is held to the lower end by missing an abstract and some formatting inconsistencies. A score of 3/4 reflects a clear, reader-friendly layout with minor omissions to address.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly written and consistently referenced, yet it tries to âoptimise muscle buildingâ through three separate domains, which dilutes precision.  While the body does circle back to each domain, the link between question and individual analyses is sometimes loose (e.g. plateau discussion not directly tied to optimisation).  Hence the question is clear but only partially focused, warranting the lower end of the 3-4 band.

Analyses compute simple numerical examples and draw logical but largely descriptive conclusions; statistical regression is presented without showing calculations of slope or RÂ² beyond the graph.  There is some linkage between model output and training advice, but reasoning relies on assumed coefficients.  Conclusions are âmostly supportedâ yet not deeply justified, corresponding to the lower end of 4-6.

Mathematical terminologyâlinear regression, exponential model, logarithmic growth, macronutrient ratioâis generally used accurately, and notation is acceptable.  Occasional imprecision (e.g. mixing units, undefined variables) prevents full consistency, placing the work comfortably in the âmostly accurateâ 3-4 band rather than the highest band.

Equations are typeset, figures are labelled, word count/title/pages are supplied.  Some formatting issues exist (inconsistent variable bolding, occasional image resolution, mis-numbered figures), yet overall layout is neat and supports comprehension, meriting a solid 3 in the top band for Presentation.

The essay applies linear, exponential, logarithmic and system-of-equations models correctly in form.  Explanations of parameters (e.g. kcal per gram, growth coefficients) show reasonable subject knowledge.  Nevertheless, derivations are superficial, coefficients lack empirical derivation and statistical treatment is minimal.  Application is mostly relevant but not consistently rigorous, fitting the mid-band.

The student clearly states a title and gives an opening description that links mathematics to muscle hypertrophy.  Headings, figures and an explicit purpose (to optimise muscle building through models) make the topic easy to follow, but the scope remains quite broad (training, nutrition, recovery, long-term growth) so communication is more adequate than exemplary.  Overall the purpose and focus are present and understandable, matching the 3-4 band.

The essay follows a logical EE structureâtitle page, contents, introduction, body sections, conclusion, bibliography.  Mathematical work is compartmentalised under clear headings that aid understanding.  Minor omissions (no abstract, some duplication of headings) keep it from the top of the band, but structure is still clear and appropriate.

Research draws on a handful of peer-reviewed studies plus invented âcase studiesâ.  Sources cited are relevant to hypertrophy, but no primary data are gathered and sample sizes/validity of âcase studiesâ are unclear.  Relevance is mostly maintained, yet depth is moderate, suiting the bottom of the 4-6 band.

The student offers a coherent narrative that compares optimised vs. non-optimised plans and acknowledges plateaus and diminishing returns.  Evaluation of limitations (fabricated data, need for machine learning) is brief and not strongly critical.  Argument is reasoned but only partially critical, aligning with band 4-6 at score 4.

Methodology is described (linear models for intensity, systems for diet, regression for recovery, exponential/log models for long-term growth).  However, data sources are vague (âcase study 1â, fabricated coefficients) and selection of parameters is not justified.  The range of mathematical tools is appropriate for AI SL, yet evidence of informed selection or validation is limited.  This matches a âmostly complete methodologyâ but not the sophistication expected for 5-6.

annotations.comment: Provide the rationale for selecting each mathematical model (linear, system of equations, exponential, logarithmic), linking each to specific variables or physiological factors.
The description of recovery modeling lacks concrete parameter values or source data, making validation of the approach difficult.
Including brief profiles of each case study (sample size, demographic, measurement protocol) would enhance the transparency and robustness of the methodology.
Underlying assumptions (e.g., linearity of recovery, constant growth coefficients) are not explicitly stated or justified, limiting critical evaluation.
The title clearly communicates the topic and purpose by specifying âOptimizing Muscle Building Using Mathematics,â setting an appropriate focus for the investigation.
The research question is explicit but too broad, combining exercise routines, nutrition, and hypertrophy optimization; narrowing the scope to one domain would improve focus.
The document correctly provides a word count under 4000 words and includes candidate details as required, ensuring compliance with formatting regulations.
The table of contents is thorough, but the use of overlined numerals for page references creates inconsistency with standard numbering formats.
An abstract is conventionally expected before the table of contents; including a succinct abstract would strengthen the structure and guide reader comprehension.
The page numbering entry in the table uses bolded math notation for â2,â which is inconsistent with other page numbers; align formatting for clarity.
The introduction offers a compelling personal narrative that demonstrates authentic engagement but should more quickly connect to the mathematical framework.
While the narrative is engaging, the introduction lacks an overview of key mathematical methods; briefly summarizing the planned models would improve focus.
Consider introducing fundamental definitions (e.g., linear regression, exponential growth) in the opening to orient the reader to the mathematical concepts used later.
The research question is restated clearly and linked to the broader inquiry, reinforcing coherence between introduction and investigation.
The overview of methods remains at a high level; justifying the choice of linear versus exponential or logarithmic modeling with context would strengthen methodology clarity.
Terminology such as âmuscle hypertrophyâ and âmTORâ is used accurately, demonstrating solid subject knowledge in physiology.
Some physiological terms lack direct citation support; defining specialized concepts (e.g., mTOR pathway) with academic references would bolster credibility.
Add a citation for the description of the mTOR mechanism to validate the molecular explanation and align with academic conventions.
Citing Terentjevâs adaptation of Ogataâs differential equations demonstrates solid engagement with locus research and enhances research quality.
The description of how Ogataâs differential equations inform threshold values is vague; more detail on derivation or parameter selection is needed.
Explain how threshold parameters (load, frequency, volume) in Terentjevâs model were determined or reference empirical studies to justify their values.
Accurate reference to linear programming in nutrition optimization shows good command of mathematical tools applied to real-world problems.
The discussion generalizes the transfer of optimization from engineering/economics to nutrition without detailing specific algorithmic applications or limitations.
To illustrate the utility of optimization, include a concise linear programming formulation (objective function, constraints) with real or sample data.
Case studies are cited without detailsâsample characteristics, statistical significance, or effect sizes; include these to improve evaluation rigor.

similarExemplars.title: Which nonlinear models best describe the population growth of Ghana, and how do these models provide insights into future trends and resource planning?

userSubmission.researchQuestion: Which nonlinear models best describe the population growth of Ghana, and how do these models provide insights into future trends and resource planning?
